title: icmpv6: lichen_internet_checksum missing NULL validation
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/l2/icmpv6.c
Lines: 64-86 (lichen_internet_checksum function)

The lichen_internet_checksum() utility function does not validate its input parameters. If called with data=NULL and len>0, it will crash with a null pointer dereference.

Compare to other functions in the same file:
- lichen_icmpv6_verify_checksum: checks NULL at line 147
- lichen_icmpv6_parse: checks NULL at lines 169-172
- lichen_icmpv6_handle: checks NULL at lines 469-472

The function is declared in the public header (icmpv6.h) and could be called by external code.

Fix: Add NULL validation at the start:
```c
uint16_t lichen_internet_checksum(const uint8_t *data, size_t len)
{
    if (data == NULL && len > 0) {
        return 0; // Or define error behavior
    }
    // ...
}
```

Note: This is lower severity (P3) because the function is primarily an internal utility and all current internal call sites pass valid data. However, as a public API function, defensive validation is best practice.
